title: Line and Block Visual
subtitle: V selects whole lines. Ctrl-V selects rectangles.
summary: V enters linewise Visual: motions extend the selection by whole lines. Ctrl-V enters blockwise Visual: the selection is a rectangle, useful for column edits.

blocks.text: {key:v} selects characters. Two more visual modes select differently-shaped regions: {key:V} for whole lines, {key:Ctrl-V} for rectangles.
From any Visual mode, press {key:v}, {key:V}, or {key:Ctrl-V} to *switch* to that mode while keeping the selection.

blocks.title: Three Modes, Three Selection Shapes
blocks.text:
Internally Vim represents selections as (anchor, cursor, *type*) where type is `char`, `line`, or `block`. The type changes how operators behave:

  • **Charwise** ({key:v}) — operators see a contiguous span of bytes from anchor to cursor.
  • **Linewise** ({key:V}) — operators see whole lines containing anchor and cursor. Column position doesn't matter.
  • **Blockwise** ({key:Ctrl-V}) — operators see a *rectangle*. Yanks come back blockwise, paste back as rectangles, and {key:I} / {key:A} work on every line of the block (see [`visual.block-insert-append`](#visual.block-insert-append)).

blocks.title: Block Mode Is the Killer Feature for Columns
blocks.text:
Block-visual on a column lets you insert, append, change, or delete *down a column* across many lines. Common uses:

  • Add `// ` to many lines: {key:Ctrl-V}-select-down-{key:I}{key:/}{key:/}{key:Space}{key:Esc}. The prefix is duplicated to every line of the block.
  • Replace a column of values: {key:Ctrl-V}-select-rectangle-{key:c}-type-replacement-{key:Esc}.
  • Delete a column of leading whitespace: {key:Ctrl-V}-select-the-leading-spaces-{key:d}.

The {key:I}/{key:A}-replicates-to-every-line trick happens *only* in block mode and *only* with capital {key:I} / {key:A} (not lowercase {key:i} / {key:a}).
